purpose: Define how a project direction change, such as moving from porting to refactoring, must be recorded and reviewed before it can affect taxonomy extensions, view tree profiles, traversal, or contract generation.

directionChangeFlow.description: A user or AI candidate states the proposed direction change and why the current project memory no longer matches the project.
Analyze affected preservation policies, improvement policies, source authority, taxonomy profile refs, and view tree profile refs.
Propose added, removed, deprecated, or renamed node/edge kinds. Do not apply them.
Propose viewpoint tree changes that would explain the new direction. Do not make them authoritative.
Present impact, risks, extension deltas, and blocked authority changes for human judgment.
A future explicit approval path may record a new project memory revision. This preview does not implement it.

blockedDirectionChanges.reason: Direction change candidates cannot self-approve project memory or extension authority.
Changing direction cannot automatically turn observed inventory into editable scope.
A direction change cannot remove forbidden flow boundaries without an explicit future review packet.

nonAuthorityBoundary: Direction changes are candidate/proposal/review artifacts only in this boundary. They do not apply profile changes, grant extension authority, change traversal, mutate graph-source, or replace human review.
